div.h1: C10K Benchmark Architecture and Expected Results
div.p: Single-threaded epoll server vs. Apache prefork at 10,000 simultaneous connections

g.text: 4 | KEEP_ALIVE | wait for next request (30s timer)
embedded in conn_t | included in 260 MB
Reduce write_buf to 8KB | => ~340 MB for 10K conns
INFEASIBLE | typical server has 32-128 GB RAM
Scheduler overhead | 10K threads × context switch cost
Failure Modes to Diagnose (if results are poor)
Connection errors | Check: ulimit -n not increased
Hangs / timeouts | Check: ET mode missing drain-to-EAGAIN
High CPU idle | Check: epoll_wait timeout = 0 (polling)
Memory OOM | Check: write_buf not freed on close
fd exhaustion | Check: conn not freed after close
Required sysctl and ulimit (both client and server)
CPU for idle conns | 0 (blocked in epoll_wait) | 10K sleeping threads
Scaling model | I/O bound by NIC | CPU/RAM bound
